★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 5/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 4/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 7.09 7.74 3.54 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 2.45 1.35 1.22 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.61 1.72 0.80 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 3.04 4.67 1.52 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 4.05 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.15 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
6.68
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
1.83
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.33
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

15.5%
annual
RN turnover
11.8%

last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
2.74
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.99
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-11-22 13 10 4 56 1 56
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-12-01 15 8 7 52 1 52
Total weighted health score
55.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (45)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

6 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
39 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. B 2024-11-22 2024-12-21
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2024-11-22 2024-12-21
0694 Provide for the safe, appropriate administration of IV fluids for a resident when needed. D 2024-11-22 2024-12-21
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-11-22 2024-12-21
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. E 2024-11-22 2024-12-21
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. D 2024-11-22 2024-12-21
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-11-22 2024-12-21
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. B 2024-11-22 2024-12-21
0909 Regularly inspect all bed frames, mattresses, and bed rails (if any) for safety; and all bed rails and mattresses must attach safely to the bed frame. E 2024-11-22 2024-12-21
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-11-22 2024-12-21
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-10-15 2024-10-30
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-10-15 2024-10-30
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. B 2023-12-01 2023-12-26
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-12-01 2023-12-26
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2023-12-01 2023-12-26
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2023-12-01 2023-12-26
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2023-12-01 2023-12-26
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2023-12-01 2023-12-26
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. B 2023-12-01 2023-12-26
0882 Designate a qualified infection preventionist to be responsible for the infection prevent and control program in the nursing home. D 2023-12-01 2023-12-26
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-11-30 2023-12-26
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2023-10-26 2023-11-26
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. B 2023-10-26 2023-11-26
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-08-01 2023-09-08
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. D 2023-06-06 2023-06-27
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2023-01-23 2023-02-14
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2023-01-23 2023-02-14
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. B 2023-01-23 2023-02-14
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-01-23 2023-02-14
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-01-23 2023-02-14

If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. D 2023-01-23 2023-02-14
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2023-01-23 2023-02-14
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2023-01-23 2023-02-14
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2023-01-23 2023-02-14
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2023-01-23 2023-02-14
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2023-01-23 2023-02-14
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2023-01-23 2023-02-14
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. D 2023-01-23 2023-02-14
0909 Regularly inspect all bed frames, mattresses, and bed rails (if any) for safety; and all bed rails and mattresses must attach safely to the bed frame. E 2023-01-23 2023-02-14
